summary: Large include-path cleanup replacing project-wide references to `<Runtime/platform.h>` with the shorter public include form `<platform.h>`. Related Runtime headers such as `<Runtime/__mem.h>`, `<Runtime/Gecko_setjmp.h>`, `<Runtime/Gecko_ExceptionPPC.h>`, `<Runtime/__init_cpp_exceptions.h>`, and `<Runtime/global_destructor_chain.h>` were also changed to root-style include names in affected source files. The change touched 297 files across MSL, Runtime, MetroTRK, Dolphin libraries, fighters, items, stages, and core melee headers. `src/Runtime/platform.h` also had its include guard renamed from `INCLUDE_RUNTIME_PLATFORM_H` to `RUNTIME_PLATFORM_H`. No PR body, review comments, or human rationale were present in the provided slice; the rationale is inferred from the title and diff as include-path standardization.
what_changed: Replaced many `#include <Runtime/platform.h>` directives with `#include <platform.h>` across headers and C files.

Moved some `platform.h` includes to the top of C files before local subsystem headers, as seen in `src/MSL/abort_exit.c`, `src/MSL/console_io.c`, `src/MSL/ctype.c`, `src/MSL/trigf.c`, `src/Runtime/Gecko_setjmp.c`, `src/Runtime/__init_cpp_exceptions.c`, `src/common_structs.h`, `src/cstring.h`, and `src/dolphin/ar/ar.c`.

Changed direct includes of Runtime-local headers to root-style include paths in several Runtime and Dolphin files, for example `<Runtime/__mem.h>` to `<__mem.h>`, `<Runtime/Gecko_setjmp.h>` to `<Gecko_setjmp.h>`, `<Runtime/Gecko_ExceptionPPC.h>` to `<Gecko_ExceptionPPC.h>`, and `<Runtime/global_destructor_chain.h>` to `<global_destructor_chain.h>`.
Renamed the include guard inside `src/Runtime/platform.h` from `INCLUDE_RUNTIME_PLATFORM_H` to `RUNTIME_PLATFORM_H`.

Updated `Cargo.lock`, including package version changes such as `melee-fix-labels` from `0.1.0` to `0.2.0`, `clap`/`clap_builder` from `4.2.1` to `4.2.2`, `syn` from `2.0.14` to `2.0.15`, and related transitive dependency changes. The slice does not explain whether this was required by the include rewrite tooling or incidental.
smart_moves: Made `platform.h` behave like a repository-wide public platform header instead of forcing consumers to know it lives under `Runtime/`.
Applied the include-path convention consistently across many subsystems in one focused PR, reducing mixed styles and future churn.
Kept most file changes mechanical and low-risk: the diff excerpt shows include lines changing without behavioral code edits.
Updated Runtime self-includes to the same public include style, which avoids special casing Runtime files differently from Dolphin, MSL, fighter, item, and stage code.
Adjusted the include guard to match the canonical logical header name `RUNTIME_PLATFORM_H`, making the guard less tied to an old include-path spelling.
decomp_lessons: For ubiquitous foundational headers, prefer a stable public include spelling such as `<platform.h>` over directory-qualified implementation paths like `<Runtime/platform.h>` when the build include paths support it.
Large mechanical include cleanups are safest when they avoid mixing in semantic code changes; this PR mostly limits itself to include directives and one include-guard rename.
When moving toward decomp-source readability, include paths should reflect the intended API boundary, not necessarily the physical directory layout.

If a header supplies primitive typedefs, compiler macros, attributes, or section annotations, include it early in files that depend on those declarations; the diff repeatedly places `<platform.h>` before subsystem headers.

Root-style Runtime helper headers such as `<__mem.h>` can make decompiled Dolphin/MSL source look less project-internal than `<Runtime/__mem.h>`, but this should be applied consistently to avoid ambiguous include conventions.

naming_conventions.meaning: Canonical include form for the Runtime platform definitions after this PR.
naming_conventions.evidence: PR title and many diff hunks replacing `<Runtime/platform.h>` with `<platform.h>`.

naming_conventions.meaning: Canonical root-style include form used in changed Dolphin CARD files instead of `<Runtime/__mem.h>`.
Runtime helper headers were included by basename from the configured include path rather than with the `Runtime/` prefix.
naming_conventions.evidence: Diff hunks in Runtime source files.

assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly-specific matching tactic is documented in the PR text or review comments.
The main matching-preservation tactic visible in the diff is mechanical include normalization without changing function bodies, data definitions, or control flow.
Putting `<platform.h>` before headers that use platform typedefs/macros may prevent compile-order issues while leaving generated code unaffected.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
No explicit reviewer rationale or requested changes are available; conclusions are based on the title and diff only.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that assume `<platform.h>` is the canonical include form.
Search for any remaining `<Runtime/platform.h>` includes after PR 775.
Check whether root-style Runtime includes like `<__mem.h>` and `<Gecko_setjmp.h>` became the long-term convention.
Investigate why `Cargo.lock` changed in this PR and whether `melee-fix-labels 0.2.0` was related to automated include rewriting.
Look for subsequent include-order cleanup PRs that build on this normalization.
